componentDescription: A component that displays rich content on hover, providing additional information or actions.

children.description: Specifies the children (React elements) that will be rendered inside the HoverCard component.

views.description: Allows for custom styling or view properties to be applied to different sub-components of the HoverCard.

container.description: Defines view-specific properties for the main container of the HoverCard.

content.description: Defines view-specific properties for the content wrapper within the HoverCard.

openDelay.description: Sets the delay in milliseconds before the HoverCard opens after the trigger is hovered.

closeDelay.description: Sets the delay in milliseconds before the HoverCard closes after the trigger is unhovered.

children.description: Specifies the children (React elements) that serve as the trigger for the HoverCard's visibility.

views.description: Allows for custom styling or view properties to be applied to different parts of the trigger.

container.description: Defines view-specific properties for the trigger's container element.

asChild.description: When true, it renders the 'children' as a direct child of the component without wrapping it in an additional DOM element.

arbitraryProps.description: Allows any additional, arbitrary props to be passed through to the component's underlying DOM element.

children.description: Specifies the children (React elements) that will be displayed within the HoverCard when it is open.

views.description: Allows for custom styling or view properties to be applied to different parts of the content.

container.description: Defines view-specific properties for the content's container element.

side.description: Determines the preferred side ('top', 'right', 'bottom', or 'left') of the trigger to render the content.

align.description: Specifies the alignment ('start', 'center', or 'end') of the content relative to the trigger along its chosen side.

sideOffset.description: Sets the distance in pixels between the content and the trigger.

arbitraryProps.description: Allows any additional, arbitrary props to be passed through to the component's underlying DOM element.
